steps.title: Use the drawback signal
steps.content: The passage says calling the novel autobiographical can be useful, but it may also make readers think Fitzgerald merely fictionalized true events. Because the passage says creativity and inventiveness are prized in art, that perception would lower the work's apparent imaginative value.

steps.title: Reject unsupported shifts
steps.content: Choice B invents a criticism of what Fitzgerald should have claimed. Choice C invents a debate among critics, and Choice D shifts to identifying real-world counterparts of characters. The sentence needs the negative effect of seeming to copy true events instead of inventing creatively. The answer is A.
